full_response:
Let S be the (unordered) sum of the two fair six–sided dice on a single roll.  
Unconditionally, S takes the values 2,…,12 with the familiar 36 equiprobable outcomes; the number of outcomes giving each sum is:

2 : 1, 3 : 2, 4 : 3, 5 : 4, 6 : 5, 7 : 6, 8 : 5, 9 : 4, 10 : 3, 11 : 2, 12 : 1.

We are told that on each of the ten rolls no 5 and no value ≥ 8 was seen.  
Hence for every one of the ten rolls the only admissible sums are

2, 3, 4, 6, 7.

Conditional probabilities.  
Let A be the event “S ∈ {2,3,4,6,7}”.  
P(A)= (1+2+3+5+6)/36 = 17/36.

For s∈{2,3,4,6,7},

P(S=s | A)= (# of outcomes giving s) /17.

Thus

• P(S = 2 | A) = 1/17  
• P(S = 3 | A) = 2/17  
• P(S = 4 | A) = 3/17  
• P(S = 6 | A) = 5/17  
• P(S = 7 | A) = 6/17.

Every one of the ten constrained rolls is i.i.d. with this distribution, so the random variable X (the sum of the ten rolls) is the sum of ten independent copies of this five-point distribution.

(For later use: E[S|A]=(2·1+3·2+4·3+6·5+7·6)/17=92/17≈5.4118, hence E[X]=10·92/17=920/17≈54.12.)

\boxed{\text{Allowed single-roll sums } \{2,3,4,6,7\}\text{ with respective conditional probabilities }1/17,\,2/17,\,3/17,\,5/17,\,6/17.}  
Confidence: 86%
extracted_answer: \text{Allowed single-roll sums } \{2,3,4,6,7\}\text{ with respective conditional probabilities }1/17,\,2/17,\,3/17,\,5/17,\,6/17.
